title: astilbe japonica var. terrestris
intro: Astilbe japonica var. terrestris, also known as mountain astilbe, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Saxifragaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and reducing fever. The health benefits of the plant include its ability to act as a diuretic, helping to reduce water retention and alleviate symptoms of urinary tract infections. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by infusing the leaves and roots in hot water, which can be used as a tea or applied topically as a poultice to promote healing and reduce inflammation.
